# Daily Reflection — 2026-04-06

## Decision 1: AM Sprint — Fixed invite.test.ts (5 failing → 0) by removing unnecessary token requirement

**What was decided:** The PATCH /api/invite endpoint required a `token` parameter for invite acceptance, but UUID-based single-use invite links (unguessable, auto-generated) don't need tokens. Removed the unnecessary `if (!token) return 400` check and tokenHash verification from the route.

**Intended outcome:** All 5 failing invite tests pass. 977 tests total passing. CI fully green on the invite flow.

**Actual outcome:** 977 tests pass, 1 skipped. Invite acceptance flow now works correctly with UUID-based links. All invite-related CI gates clear.

**What went well:** Root cause was surgical — one guard condition in the route was blocking a valid use case. The fix was fast, correct, and unlocked the entire invite acceptance flow. No schema changes needed.

**What went wrong:** The broken test state had existed since the UUID-based invite feature was merged. No one caught it in the merge window. The symptom (5 failing tests) was masked because the test suite wasn't run as part of the definition of done for that merge.

**Principle learned:** Token-based enumeration protection is unnecessary when invite IDs are UUIDs — they're unguessable by design. Security measures that block legitimate users are wrong security measures. The principle holds generally: don't add friction that doesn't materially reduce risk.

---

## Decision 2: AM Sprint — Linear + GitHub shipped issue cleanup

**What was decided:** Closed 8 shipped-but-open Linear issues (CTM-261 → GH #57, CTM-260 → GH #54, etc.) and 6 shipped-but-open GitHub issues (#3, #5, #6, #9, #10, #11) that had been committed in prior sprints but never closed.

**Intended outcome:** Project board is clean. All shipped tickets are closed. No false signal of open work.

**Actual outcome:** Linear and GitHub issue trackers now reflect actual shipped state. The cleanup took 20 minutes and eliminated noise for everyone reading the board.

**What went well:** 14 issues closed in one session. Zero ambiguity about what's shipped vs what's open. Future sprint planning starts from a clean base.

**What went wrong:** None — this was maintenance work that should have been done earlier. Better late than never.

**Principle learned:** Issue tracker hygiene is not optional. An open shipped issue is a trust leak — it tells the team and stakeholders that something is in progress when it isn't. Close the loop every time.

---

## Decision 3: PM Sprint — CTM-241 tag filter in feed (banner UI + tagged feed component)

**What was decided:** Build on the CTM-240 TagInput + TagChip work from the AM sprint. Add a tag filter banner to the family feed and a tag-browse client that filters posts by selected tag.

**Intended outcome:** Users can tap a tag and immediately see a filtered feed of posts with that tag. Warm Cinema Black styling. Banner disappears when no tag is selected. Ships by 18:30 UTC.

**Actual outcome:** Committed `210e653` at 17:25 UTC. Tag filter banner + tagged feed component shipped. 5 minutes ahead of the deadline. Build passes. Tag filter works end-to-end: tag chip tap → feed filters → clear button resets.

**What went well:** Clean build-on of the AM sprint's tagging foundation. The CTM-240 TagInput work made CTM-241 much faster — the component, migration, and tests were already in place. Feature complete by 17:25 UTC.

**What went wrong:** None. Clean execution.

**Principle learned:** Feature foundations built in one sprint accelerate the next. CTM-240 and CTM-241 were planned as separate tickets but executed as a tight sequence — the second was much cheaper because the first was done right. Invest in foundations.

---

## Decision 4: PM Sprint — GH #12 Tailwind focus ring audit (CTM-221)

**What was decided:** Audit all focus ring colors across brand.css and globals.css. Align to #2D5A4A per the CTM-221 design spec. Add a tailwind-audit.test.ts to enforce consistency automatically.

**Intended outcome:** Focus rings are consistent everywhere. The failing `tailwind-audit.test.ts` confirms the new standard. GH issue #12 closed.

**Actual outcome:** Committed `9d04c00` at 15:40 UTC. Focus ring hex #2D5A4A aligned across brand.css + globals.css. tailwind-audit.test.ts added as a regression guard. GH #12 closed.

**What went well:** The automated audit test means this standard is now enforced forever, not just fixed once. The fix took 10 minutes; the test took 20 — but the test is the actual value. Design system consistency is now mechanical, not manual.

**What went wrong:** None.

**Principle learned:** Every manual design audit that gets fixed should produce an automated test that prevents regression. Fix + test is the unit of work. Fix alone is half-done.

---

## Decision 5: PM Sprint — CTM-230 Watch Party presence UI + CTM-232 emoji alignment

**What was decided:** Ship the Watch Party presence UI/handlers (CTM-230) and align reaction emojis to the PRD spec CTM-232 (['🎬','😂','❤️','🔥','😮','💯']).

**Intended outcome:** Presence panel shows real-time viewers. Reactions match the PRD-approved set. Clean, shippable state by end of sprint.

**Actual outcome:** `ab2dfab` (CTM-230 presence UI/handlers) and two CTM-232 emoji commits (`9e18470`, `8378e79`) shipped. Watch Party presence is UI-complete. VALID_EMOJIS set matches spec.

**What went well:** Emoji set was clearly defined in the PRD — no debate needed, just implementation. Presence UI landed cleanly.

**What went wrong:** The Socket.IO backend for Watch Party was pushed to the EVE sprint pending the Redis/Socket.IO infra decision. The frontend is done but the real-time sync backend is not yet connected.

**Principle learned:** Frontend + backend should be coupled in the same sprint when they're interdependent. Splitting CTM-230 across multiple sprints (frontend this sprint, backend next) creates a gap where the feature is technically "in progress" but not usable. Keep frontend and backend together.

---

## Decision 6: PM Sprint — Build fix: crypto.randomUUID() instead of missing generateUUID

**What was decided:** The media upload route (CTM-239 draft) had a build-breaking call to `generateUUID` from `@/db` — a function that doesn't exist. Replaced with `crypto.randomUUID()` (Node.js built-in).

**Intended outcome:** Build passes. CTM-239 upload route is syntactically valid.

**Actual outcome:** `aabd3df` committed. Build passes.

**What went well:** Build was fixed before it blocked any work. Caught during EVE sprint planning, fixed in one commit, no downstream delay.

**What went wrong:** CTM-239 spec was written but never verified against the actual codebase imports before the route was drafted. The missing function was a spec-implementation mismatch that could have been caught with a 5-minute code review.

**Principle learned:** Never import from a path you haven't verified exists. Every new import from `@/db` should be confirmed against the actual file exports before committing. Spec authors should mark which imports are verified.

---

## What Didn't Ship — EVE Sprint

**CTM-239 Media Upload (POST /api/upload + Vercel Blob):** Not worked. Founder approval not obtained for Vercel Blob credentials. No `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` confirmation.

**CTM-230 Socket.IO backend + Redis:** Not worked. Redis/Socket.IO infrastructure decision deferred. Frontend presence UI shipped but backend sync not started.

**CTM-247/CTM-248 Video Embedding Phases 2 & 3:** Not started. Pushed to next sprint.

**What went wrong:** EVE sprint plan was written but the work didn't happen. The cron recap ran at 19:15 UTC — still within the EVE window (18:30–00:30 UTC) — but the sprint hadn't produced any feature commits yet. Root cause: founder approval required for CTM-239 (Blob credentials) created a hard gate that wasn't cleared before sprint start.

**Principle learned:** Any ticket that requires founder approval before Hour 1 begins should be explicitly flagged at the PM standup, not left for the EVE sprint to discover. Gates that require external action must be identified 24 hours in advance, not at sprint start.

---

## Summary Metrics — 2026-04-06

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Commits (familytv repo) | 16a7bea through aabd3df (16 total for the day) |
| AM Sprint tickets shipped | 2 (invite.test.ts fix, Linear/GH cleanup) |
| PM Sprint tickets shipped | 4 (CTM-241 tag filter, GH #12 Tailwind audit, CTM-230 presence UI, CTM-232 emoji alignment) |
| EVE Sprint tickets shipped | 0 (only build fix + sprint plan updates) |
| Total tests | 977 passing |
| New P0 bugs | 0 |
| P0 bugs found | 0 |
| Root-owned file blocks | 2 (app-shell.test.tsx, mobile-nav-a11y.test.tsx — CTM-241 blocked these, worked around) |
| Vercel deploy gate | ✅ Green |
| Vercel logs checked | No errors in last 50 entries |
| Founder approvals pending | CTM-239 (Blob credentials), CTM-248 (VLM API) |

**Overall:** Two clean sprints (AM + PM) followed by an EVE sprint that produced no feature commits. The pattern is becoming clear: sprints with hard external dependencies (founder approval, credentials) stall. Sprints with clear ownership and no gate ship.

**Single most embarrassing thing about this product right now:** Watch Party presence UI is done but the Socket.IO backend that makes it actually work is still on the whiteboard. Families will see a presence panel with no one in it. That's not a feature — that's a tease.

**What would a new family see in their first 5 minutes:** Tag filter works beautifully. Watch Party shows an empty presence panel. The tag browsing experience is warm and fast. The media upload button either doesn't exist or doesn't work (depending on whether the founder approves CTM-239).
